points.text: COVID-19 can lead to serious complications and death, unlike the common cold or flu, which are generally less severe.
COVID-19 symptoms can include loss of taste or smell, which is not typical for the common cold or flu.
COVID-19 spreads primarily through respiratory droplets, similar to the flu, but can also spread from asymptomatic individuals.
COVID-19 can cause long-term symptoms, known as post-COVID-19 syndrome, which is still being studied.
The virus causing COVID-19, SARS-CoV-2, is genetically distinct from the viruses that cause the common cold and flu.
COVID-19 is caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus, while the common cold and flu are caused by different viruses.
Symptoms of COVID-19 can range from mild to severe, similar to flu and cold symptoms, requiring testing for confirmation.
COVID-19 can lead to serious complications like multisystem inflammatory syndrome in children, which is not typical for colds or flu.
COVID-19 symptoms include loss of taste or smell, which are not common in cold or flu cases.
COVID-19 can cause severe illness in people with pre-existing conditions, while colds and flu generally have milder outcomes.
